message:
fix(limits): raise pack loader + wa.send caps to match operator budget (ft-05hfm follow-up)

Follow-up to e8397747. The initial caps (1 MiB for both) were set
from a secure-minimum posture; operator guidance raises them to the
budget documented in the closing directive:

- MAX_PACK_BYTES: 1 MiB → 16 MiB (pattern-pack loader)
- MAX_SEND_TEXT_BYTES: 1 MiB → 4 MiB (wa.send text payload)

16 MiB pack cap is four orders of magnitude above the largest
built-in pack (still rejects adversarial `.ft/patterns/*.yaml`
payloads long before the YAML deserializer sees them). 4 MiB send
cap is ~40x the largest typical paste, high enough to accept
uncommon-but-valid bulk-input scenarios (e.g. paste of a long
generated artifact) while still bounding the OOM vector through
the injector → policy → wezterm dispatch pipeline.

## Tests

`max_send_text_bytes_is_one_mib` renamed to `max_send_text_bytes_
is_four_mib` and re-pinned against `4 * 1024 * 1024`. The
boundary-sanity test (`max_send_text_bytes_exceeds_typical_paste`)
continues to pass: 4 MiB > 100 KiB typical-paste threshold.

Pack loader tests unchanged — they exercise the oversize-reject
path with `MAX_PACK_BYTES + 64` bytes, which scales correctly
with the new 16 MiB ceiling.

## Related

- e8397747 — initial ft-05hfm fix at 1 MiB caps.
- ft-05hfm bead closure — carries the full fix narrative.
